Here's a description of what's visible in the screenshot:

The screen shows an instructional coding video interface, likely using a platform like Replit or similar. The title "Randomness and Noise" is prominently displayed in green text at the top, with a "1" written beside it. A simple graph is shown on the black background; it consists of a horizontal red line at y=0 and a vertical red line extending upwards from the x-axis. 

The UI includes icons for running, debugging, and other coding tools on the left side (arrow, square, "A", chat bubble, downward arrow). The bottom toolbar has icons for fullscreen, grid view, settings, and a "Stop" button. No code editor is visible in this screenshot.

Here's a description of what's visible in the screenshot:

The screen shows an instructional coding video interface, likely for a tool like Excalidraw or similar. A green line graph is prominently displayed on a black background, resembling a random walk or noisy signal. The x-axis represents time (indicated by an arrow), and the y-axis is labeled "0" at the bottom. Above the graph, text reads "Randomness and Noise (xOff 1 nowe(0))". A vertical red line is drawn at the beginning of the graph, with a "1" next to it.

There are UI elements on the left side including icons for selection, text, and shapes. The bottom of the screen has buttons for grid view, undo/redo, and a "Stop" button. No code editor is visible in this screenshot.

Text at the top reads "Randomness and Noise (xOff nowe(0.00) = 0.4". A yellow circle highlights a point on the graph, and a '1' is written to its left.
